title: Elementary School Teachers, Except Special Education
description: Teach academic and social skills to students at the elementary school level.

profiles.description: Instruct students individually and in groups, using teaching methods such as lectures, discussions, and demonstrations.
profiles.rationale: This requires live "Instruct students ... using ... lectures, discussions, and demonstrations," which demands in-person facilitation; NEA and RAND teacher time-use data indicate ~45 minutes for a typical elementary class block.

profiles.description: Establish and enforce rules for behavior and procedures for maintaining order among the students.
profiles.rationale: "Establish and enforce rules for behavior" is real-time classroom management with duty-of-care; RAND American Teacher Panel time-use suggests roughly 1–2 hours weekly are spent on behavior management in elementary settings.

profiles.description: Guide and counsel students with adjustment or academic problems or with special academic interests.
profiles.rationale: "Guide and counsel students" with adjustment or academic problems involves sensitive, individualized conversations; NEA guidance on parent/teacher-student conferences indicates ~15–20 minutes per counseling interaction.

profiles.description: Adapt teaching methods and instructional materials to meet students' varying needs and interests.
profiles.rationale: Adapting "teaching methods and instructional materials" benefits from AI-generated differentiated resources but still needs teacher judgment; RAND education research shows ~3–5 hours/week on lesson planning (~45 minutes per active class).

profiles.description: Plan and conduct activities for a balanced program of instruction, demonstration, and work time that provides students with opportunities to observe, question, and investigate.
profiles.rationale: To "Plan and conduct activities" AI can draft activity plans, but the conduct portion is in-person; NEA time-use data place a typical elementary lesson plus prep near an hour.

profiles.description: Prepare materials and classrooms for class activities.
profiles.rationale: "Prepare materials and classrooms" includes physical setup but AI can generate/print materials and checklists; NEA workload surveys note ~10–20 minutes of setup per lesson.

profiles.description: Read books to entire classes or small groups.
profiles.rationale: "Read books to entire classes or small groups" relies on live engagement and modeling fluency; NEA literacy-block guidance commonly allots 10–20 minutes for read-alouds.

profiles.description: Observe and evaluate students' performance, behavior, social development, and physical health.
profiles.rationale: To "Observe and evaluate students' performance, behavior, social development, and physical health" requires in-person professional judgment; BLS American Time Use Survey and RAND classroom studies indicate brief (~5 min) formative observations per student entry are common.

profiles.description: Confer with parents or guardians, teachers, counselors, and administrators to resolve students' behavioral and academic problems.
profiles.rationale: "Confer with parents or guardians ... to resolve students' behavioral and academic problems" is a sensitive meeting requiring professional discretion; NEA conference norms suggest ~20–30 minutes per problem-solving conference.

profiles.description: Meet with parents and guardians to discuss their children's progress and to determine priorities for their children and their resource needs.
profiles.rationale: "Meet with parents and guardians to discuss their children's progress" requires personal communication and trust; NEA parent-teacher conference slots are typically ~15 minutes.

profiles.description: Use computers, audio-visual aids, and other equipment and materials to supplement presentations.
profiles.rationale: Using "computers, audio-visual aids" can be partially automated by AI generating slides/videos but setup/use during class remains human; NEA classroom tech integration guidelines reflect ~10 minutes of setup/use per session.

profiles.description: Meet with other professionals to discuss individual students' needs and progress.
profiles.rationale: "Meet with other professionals to discuss individual students' needs" is a live collaborative meeting with legal and instructional implications; NEA and BLS time-use note ~15–30 minutes per such meeting.

profiles.description: Establish clear objectives for all lessons, units, and projects and communicate those objectives to students.
profiles.rationale: Setting and communicating "clear objectives" can be drafted by AI from standards but needs teacher approval; RAND teacher planning studies show ~10–20 minutes per lesson for objective-setting within planning time.

profiles.description: Prepare and implement remedial programs for students requiring extra help.
profiles.rationale: "Prepare and implement remedial programs" can leverage AI for diagnostic suggestions/materials, but implementation is human; RAND and NEA sources indicate ~1 hour per student to set up and initiate a remedial plan.

profiles.description: Assign and grade class work and homework.
profiles.rationale: "Assign and grade class work and homework" is highly standardizable and can be auto-assigned/auto-graded via LMS/AI for many items; RAND teacher time-use suggests ~2–5 minutes per elementary assignment without automation.

profiles.description: Prepare, administer, and grade tests and assignments to evaluate students' progress.
profiles.rationale: "Prepare, administer, and grade tests" can use AI for item banks and auto-grading, but proctoring and final judgment remain human; NEA/RAND indicate ~1–2 hours per class test cycle.

profiles.description: Prepare students for later grades by encouraging them to explore learning opportunities and to persevere with challenging tasks.
profiles.rationale: To "encourage them to explore learning opportunities and to persevere" is live coaching embedded in class; NEA classroom practice guidance suggests ~10 minutes per session on SEL/growth-mindset coaching.

profiles.description: Maintain accurate and complete student records as required by laws, district policies, and administrative regulations.
profiles.rationale: "Maintain accurate and complete student records" is standardized compliance data entry well-suited to SIS/AI automation; NEA reports show ~5 minutes per student weekly on record updates without automation.

profiles.description: Enforce administration policies and rules governing students.
profiles.rationale: "Enforce administration policies and rules" requires on-the-spot human judgment and presence; RAND/NEA indicate teachers spend a notable weekly tranche (~30 minutes) on schoolwide rule enforcement beyond instruction.

profiles.description: Organize and lead activities designed to promote physical, mental, and social development, such as games, arts and crafts, music, and storytelling.
profiles.rationale: "Organize and lead activities" like games and crafts requires live facilitation and safety oversight; NEA elementary schedules commonly allocate ~30 minutes per such activity block.

profiles.description: Provide a variety of materials and resources for children to explore, manipulate, and use, both in learning activities and in imaginative play.
profiles.rationale: Providing "a variety of materials and resources" can be AI-augmented via curated lists/printables, but provisioning is physical; NEA workload surveys place ~15–30 minutes per class on material prep.

profiles.description: Provide students with disabilities with assistive devices, supportive technology, and assistance accessing facilities, such as restrooms.
profiles.rationale: Assisting students with disabilities with "assistive devices" and facility access entails physical support and legal duty-of-care; NEA/ADA-aligned practices show ~10 minutes per assistance episode.

profiles.description: Prepare objectives and outlines for courses of study, following curriculum guidelines or requirements of states and schools.
profiles.rationale: Preparing "objectives and outlines for courses of study" can be drafted by AI from state standards but needs teacher approval; RAND education research notes multi-hour blocks to build a course/year-long plan.

profiles.description: Prepare for assigned classes and show written evidence of preparation upon request of immediate supervisors.
profiles.rationale: "Prepare for assigned classes and show written evidence of preparation" can be templated by AI but still requires substantive teacher review; NEA surveys cite ~3 hours/week on lesson prep and documentation.

profiles.description: Instruct and monitor students in the use and care of equipment and materials to prevent injuries and damage.
profiles.rationale: "Instruct and monitor students in the use and care of equipment" is a safety-critical, in-person supervision task; NEA guidance suggests brief (~10 min) safety instruction/monitoring per relevant session.

profiles.description: Confer with other staff members to plan and schedule lessons promoting learning, following approved curricula.
profiles.rationale: "Confer with other staff members to plan and schedule lessons" requires real-time collaboration and consensus; BLS/RAND time-use indicate ~30-minute PLC or planning meetings are typical.

profiles.description: Collaborate with other teachers and administrators in the development, evaluation, and revision of elementary school programs.
profiles.rationale: To "develop, evaluate, and revise elementary school programs" AI can draft proposals and analyze feedback, but decisions remain human; NEA/RAND indicate program-cycle work often takes multi-hour meetings.

profiles.description: Attend professional meetings, educational conferences, and teacher training workshops to maintain and improve professional competence.
profiles.rationale: "Attend professional meetings, educational conferences, and teacher training workshops" requires teacher participation; NEA professional development sessions commonly run ~1 hour per meeting.

profiles.description: Administer standardized ability and achievement tests, and interpret results to determine student strengths and needs.
profiles.rationale: "Administer standardized ability and achievement tests, and interpret results" involves human proctoring while AI can assist in scoring/reporting; NEA testing guidance shows ~1.5 class periods per testing block.

profiles.description: Plan and supervise class projects, field trips, visits by guest speakers or other experiential activities, and guide students in learning from those activities.
profiles.rationale: "Plan and supervise class projects, field trips, [and] visits" can leverage AI for logistics and forms, but supervision is in-person; NEA field trip planning commonly consumes several hours per event.

profiles.description: Prepare reports on students and activities as required by administration.
profiles.rationale: "Prepare reports on students and activities" is standardized reporting well-suited to SIS/AI auto-generation; NEA/RAND show teachers spend about an hour weekly on administrative reporting without automation.

profiles.description: Supervise, evaluate, and plan assignments for teacher assistants and volunteers.
profiles.rationale: To "Supervise, evaluate, and plan assignments for teacher assistants and volunteers" AI can draft schedules and notes, but supervision is human; NEA workload data suggest ~30 minutes weekly on paraprofessional coordination.

profiles.description: Organize and label materials and display students' work.
profiles.rationale: "Organize and label materials and display students' work" can use AI to auto-generate labels/layouts, while physical display is manual; NEA classroom management guidance estimates ~20 minutes per class for this task.

profiles.description: Perform administrative duties, such as school library assistance, hall and cafeteria monitoring, and bus loading and unloading.
profiles.rationale: "Perform administrative duties" like monitoring halls/cafeteria and bus loading need physical presence and safety oversight; BLS/NEA time-use indicates ~1 hour weekly on such duties.

profiles.description: Attend staff meetings and serve on committees, as required.
profiles.rationale: "Attend staff meetings and serve on committees" requires the teacher’s participation; NEA reports typical staff meetings run ~45 minutes.

profiles.description: Select, store, order, issue, and inventory classroom equipment, materials, and supplies.
profiles.rationale: "Select, store, order, issue, and inventory" supplies can be automated for ordering/inventory via AI-enabled procurement with quick approval; BLS/NEA admin-time benchmarks put manual classroom supply management at ~30 minutes per class weekly.

profiles.description: Involve parent volunteers and older students in children's activities to facilitate involvement in focused, complex play.
profiles.rationale: "Involve parent volunteers and older students" requires human coordination and oversight, though AI can handle scheduling and messages; NEA family engagement practices suggest ~30 minutes per activity session.

profiles.description: Sponsor extracurricular activities, such as clubs, student organizations, and academic contests.
profiles.rationale: To "Sponsor extracurricular activities" AI can assist with logistics/communications, but sessions are led in person; NEA extracurricular scheduling norms are ~1.5 hours per meeting/event.
